response.justification: Bogdan is a capable polyglot engineer with strong software craftsmanship fundamentals, but he is a poor fit for this role. The most critical disqualifier is the near-total absence of C++ experience — the role requires 8 years, while his resume shows roughly 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project. Beyond the C++ gap, he has never held a formal technical leadership position, lacks cloud platform architecture depth, and his primary stack (TypeScript, DLang, Node.js) is unaligned with the role's core technical requirements.
strengths: Consistent application of software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across multiple roles
Distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency requirements
Active knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, conference talks, and open-source project leadership
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months) versus the mandatory 8-year requirement — the single largest disqualifier
No formal technical leadership role; all positions are individual contributor with informal mentoring at best
Minimal cloud infrastructure or platform architecture depth; AWS CDK usage at Optilyz is the only cloud signal and is at the application layer, not platform level
